titleStmt.title: Letter to Piaras Béaslaí from Tomás Ó Máille, 5 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Tomás Ó Máille

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter written in Irish to Piaras Béaslaí from Tomás Ó Máille on suggestions for Irish Language Plays for performance in University College Galway's St. Patrick's Day celebrations.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Coláiste na h-Iolsgoile
i nGaillimh. 5/1/1916. A Chara, Támuid ag brath drama Gaedhilge
a chur ar bun arís i mbliadhna
le haghaidh La'il' Padraig annseo.
B'fhéidir go dtiocfadh leat-sa má 'sé
do thoil é liosta den chuid
is fearr atá ar eolus agat
atá i gceist anois a chur chugam. Maidir
leis na cinn Chonnachtacha tá
an chuid is fearr ar fad caithte
againn. Ba mhaith liom go
n-abrótha cé bhfuighfear an chuid
a mholfas tú. Tá súil agam go gcuirfe tú
freagradh chugam go luath,
mar ní mór dhúinn a dhul
a' tosaigh chomh luath a's
thiocfas na mic léighinn le
chéile. Go leige Dia slán thar
an mbliadhain thú & go mbu
seacht fearr a bhéas tú. Mise,
Tomás Ó Máille
do chara. University College
in Galway. 5/1/1916 My dear friend, We intend to put on a play here in Irish again this year for St. Patrick’s Day. Perhaps, if you wish, you might send me a list of the best ones that are currently drawing attention. Regarding the Connaught ones, we have done all the best. I would be grateful if you could say where those you recommend can be found. I hope you will respond soon, as we must start as soon as the students assemble. May God bring you safely through the year and may you be seven times better. Yours truly,
Tomás Ó Máille
Your friend.
